{{Quote|Got a spark, pal?|Moranda to Garm Bel Iblis|<ref name="Interlude at Darkknell" />}}
'''Moranda Savich''', a [[Human/Legends|Human]] woman, was a professional thief, [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]], and master of disguise who worked independently and in association with [[Talon Karrde]]'s [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] [[Car'das/Legends|organization]]. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], she helped the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] get information on the construction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]]. In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], though she was old enough to be a great-grandmother, she played an active role in Karrde's efforts to assist the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] during the [[Caamas Document]] crisis.

==Biography==
===Activities on Darkknell===
In 0 BBY, Savich was on the run from [[Corellian Security Force]]. CorSec agent [[Hal Horn]] had tracked her down on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], [[Kreeling/Legends|Kreeling]], [[Dorsis]], and [[Mantarran]], but she evaded him on each of those worlds. While on [[Darkknell/Legends|Darkknell]], she impulsively stole a [[Datacard/Legends|datapack]] from a patron at the [[Continuum Void]] [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe]] on [[Darkknell/Legends|Darkknell]], unaware that he was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] defector planning to give the datapack to a Rebel contact. Once she realized that she had stolen encrypted [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] files, and that [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] agents were on the trail of the data (though not on her trail specifically), she slipped the [[Datacard/Legends|datacards]] into the window well of an Imperial agent's rented [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]]. After she had done this, however, Hal Horn and Intelligence Agent [[Ysanne Isard]] spotted her. When she turned to run, the first agent ([[Trabler]]) shot her on Isard's orders.<ref name="Interlude at Darkknell" />

Believing her dead, Isard, Horn, and Trabler left to pursue the Rebel agents they still thought had the datapack. Savich was only wounded, however, and was taken to a safehouse for medical treatment by [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]], the intended recipient of the datapack. She agreed, for a price of 50,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], to work with Bel Iblis to steal the datacards back. She did this by calling Darkknell's security forces with false tips, hoping to draw Isard into the open. With this accomplished, she used her disguise talents to impersonate an old friend of Horn's, and her pickpocketing skills to steal Isard's identification. She then had Isard arrested by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Nyroska]] of the [[Darkknell Defense Agency]], while claiming to be Isard herself. By the time the local authorities realized they had made a mistake, Bel Iblis had retrieved the datacards, and he and Savich had separately left the planet.<ref name="Interlude at Darkknell" />

===Activities on Bothawui===
Sometime after her time on Darkknell, Savich put her talents to work for [[Talon Karrde]]'s organization. While doing courier work for Karrde on [[Cejansij]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] helped her and her [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] bodyguard [[Plakhmirakh]] fight off thugs hired by a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] syndicate. (Skywalker was on Cejansij to investigate the source of [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] who had appeared in certain [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] groups.)<ref name="Specter of the Past" />

Soon afterward, Karrde sent Savich to [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]] to investigate the anti-Bothan terrorist organization calling itself "[[Vengeance (organization)|Vengeance]]." While in the planetary capital of [[Drev'starn]], she met up with [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] (son of the late Hal Horn) and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], who had been sent on a similar mission for the New Republic by Garm Bel Iblis. Together, they had some success in tracking down the [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] cell which was behind Vengeance's activities. However, Antilles and Horn were called off their mission in order to join the rest of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], which was about to join the rest of the New Republic task force for the [[Battle of Yaga Minor (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Yaga Minor]].<ref name="Vision of the Future">''[[Vision of the Future]]''</ref>

Though her criminal past made her unable to alert the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] authorities, Savich continued to harass the Imperials. Despite starting a fire in [[Exoticalia Pet Emporium|Exoticalia's Pet Emporium]], the Drev'starn pet shop the Imperials were using as a front, "Vengeance" did not put a stop to their plans. Bothawui's [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shields]] were still sabotaged, and a [[Battle of Bothawui (Galactic Civil War)|battle at Bothawui]] ensued. However, thanks in part to her efforts, the Imperial timetable was disrupted, one of several factors which led to the [[Imperial Remnant]]'s defeat.<ref name="Vision of the Future" />

==Personality and traits==
Savich was fond of [[Gralish liqueur]], which she was often seen sipping during her adventures on Darkknell<ref name="Interlude at Darkknell" /> and Bothawui.<ref name="Vision of the Future" /> She was also known for smoking [[Cigarra/Legends|cigarras]] in her early days.<ref name="Interlude at Darkknell" />

Savich's ship, the ''[[Fingertip Express]]'', was a [[Sydon Vehicle Works|Sydon]] [[MRX-BR Pacifier]] [[Scout ship/Legends|scout vessel]] sold as Imperial surplus.<ref name="Vision of the Future" />
